title: Episode 11
wikitext:
[]: Episode 11.             Quote Post by J » Tue Sep 27, 2005 2:55 pm Okay so, i didnt see any topic of this( or then im just blind) Who hmm 'attacked' Geofront in that episode and shutdown the powersupply? I didnt see any answers in http://www.evaotaku.com/ So i wondering that if people here knew... Myself i think it was either Kaji or terrorists.

[]:             Quote Post by TsubasaNoKoikokoro » Tue Sep 27, 2005 3:17 pm Hmm? There are two common views: 1. Matariel. 2. Seele. 3. The goverment. It was not Kaji that much we know for sure.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Sep 27, 2005 3:17 pm It's kind of implied that it was Kaji. The idea was that Seele was able to figure out a lot of the layout and organizational systems of Nerv by observing the way in which Nerv got everything back up. This is the very first sign of what Seele is planning in The End of Evangelion. It is never experessly stated that it was Kaji, but logically, who else could it have been?

[]:             Quote Post by TsubasaNoKoikokoro » Tue Sep 27, 2005 3:33 pm I supose, I mean Kaji did run away from the site afterwords. But I still don't think it was him.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Sep 27, 2005 3:40 pm Yes I suppose it was most likely Kaji working for SEELE, but lets not forget his real motivation...... Money.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Tue Sep 27, 2005 3:52 pm Money were do they hint he's being paid craps loads of money?

[]:             Quote Post by TsubasaNoKoikokoro » Tue Sep 27, 2005 3:53 pm Well he is a triplle agent, almost a mercenary. They work for tons of money.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Tue Sep 27, 2005 4:19 pm Tsu-chan wrote: Well he is a triplle agent, almost a mercenary. They work for tons of money.

[]:             Quote Post by sachiel » Tue Sep 27, 2005 4:34 pm Er, I don't think Kaji was doing it all for the money. That's not in his character. In the manga, Yoshiyuki Sadamoto goes into his background and we find out that after what had happened to his friends and his brother, Kaji wanted the truth . In the anime, we don't get into it as much, as you said, but I think we can assume the same thing. It probably was Kaji who shut the power off, though. That much I can agree with..

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue Sep 27, 2005 4:53 pm I was being facetious. Getting stuck in the elevator with Misato was his real motive. If he even did anyhing.

[]:             Quote Post by wrack » Tue Sep 27, 2005 5:15 pm Oh it was Gendo. He didn't want to seem rude by cutting his son off, so he cut all the electricity then. This ended up backfiring though, but it was quickly explained with "Erm, it was in the scenario all right."

[]:             Quote Post by sachiel » Tue Sep 27, 2005 5:40 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: I was being facetious. Getting stuck in the elevator with Misato was his real motive.
